channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Thousands survived a brutal gang attack in Haiti that killed 70. Now they face an uncertain future
item.description: PONT-SONDÉ, Haiti (AP) — Under the cover of night, dozens of gang members crept toward the small town of Pont-Sondé in central Haiti armed with knives and assault rifles as families slept.
item.dc:creator: PIERRE-RICHARD LUXAMA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No evidence of major fuel spill on Samoan reef where New Zealand navy ship sank
item.description: Samoa’s deputy prime minister says there's no evidence of a major fuel spill on a Samoan reef after a New Zealand navy ship sank.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vigils, protests around the world commemorate the anniversary of Hamas attack on Israel
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Commemorations and protests unfolded across the world on Monday to mark the anniversary of the Oct.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, TASSANEE VEJPONGSA and JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Iran’s missiles strike Israel, and a deadly terror attack in Tel Aviv
item.description: On the same day Iran attacked Israel with some 181 missiles last week, a terrorist attack saw 7 innocent Israelis murdered in cold blood by two Palestinians in the Tel Aviv area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tunisia's Kais Saied wins landslide reelection, entrenching his power in Arab Spring's birthplace
item.description: Tunisia’s President Kais Saied has won reelection in a landslide victory after a campaign that saw opponents jailed alongside journalists, activists and attorneys.
item.dc:creator: Bouazza Ben Bouazza And Annie Risemberg, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Watch: Harris, Emhoff mark anniversary of Oct. 7 attacks
item.description: Vice President Harris and second gentleman Doug Emhoff on Monday marked the first anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks by Hamas on Israel. The two were expected to plant a memorial tree at the vice president’s residence, in addition to delivering remarks to the press.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Ukraine strikes a Russian oil hub as Zelenskyy says the war is in 'a very important phase'
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s military said it struck a major oil terminal Monday in Crimea that provides fuel for Russia’s war effort as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said the war has entered a key phase.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian arms dealer Viktor Bout, who was traded for Brittney Griner, to sell weapons to Iran-backed Houthis
item.description: Russian "Merchant of Death" Viktor Bout has reportedly resumed his role as an arms dealer following his December 2022 release from U.S. custody.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Saving lives on 'Death Street,' how an Israeli kindergarten teacher became a battlefield hero on October 7
item.description: When Hamas terrorists attacked her hometown in Southern Israel, Tali Hadad a kindergarten teacher, leapt into action to save 13 people, including her own wounded son.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Samoa warns of 'highly probable' fuel spill after New Zealand navy ship caught fire and sank
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A fuel spill is “highly probable” after a New Zealand navy ship grounded, caught fire and sank off the coast of Samoa, the Pacific island nation's acting prime minister said late Sunday. All 75 people on board the HMNZS Manawanui were taken to safety on life boats.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan and Georgia Arab American voters sour on Harris
item.description: Vice President Harris is facing growing signs that Arab American and Muslim voters are souring on her in the key battlegrounds of Michigan and Georgia as anger rises over the expanding conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Yash Roy, The Hill

item.title: The mayor of a state capital in Mexico is killed less than 1 week after he took office
item.description: Relatives have buried the mayor of a state capital in southern Mexico who was killed less than one week after he took office.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An explosion outside the Karachi, Pakistan, airport kills 2 workers from China and injures others
item.description: KARACHI, Pakistan (AP) — A massive blast outside the Karachi airport in southern Pakistan killed two workers from China on Sunday and injured at least eight others in the latest deadly attack on Chinese in the country.
item.dc:creator: ADIL JAWAD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New film documents Oct. 7 attacks after first anniversary
item.description: Pierre Rehov, speaking from Ashdod, Israel, said he was disturbed by reactions that seemed to excuse the attack.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Israel-Hamas war will affect generations: Humanitarian worker
item.description: Since Israel's offensive began after the October 7 attacks, the death toll in Gaza is estimated at 41,800, according to the Palestinian Health Authority.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Biden orders 500 more active-duty troops to North Carolina to aid in Helene recovery
item.description: President Biden ordered the Department of Defense to move an additional 500 active-duty troops into western North Carolina to aid in the recovery efforts following Hurricane Helene, the White House announced Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Iran lifts flight restrictions ahead of schedule after grounding all flights for 'operational restrictions'
item.description: Flight restrictions were lifted in Iran after safe conditions were confirmed, state media reported on Monday, shortening the period of flight cancellations announced earlier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Under fire: Israel's Iron Dome winning but pressure's on as conflict with Iran escalates
item.description: As the conflict in the Middle East intensifies, Iron Dome and Israel's other air defense systems have been continually challenged from prolonged use and evolving threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A strike on a mosque kills at least 19, Palestinian officials say, as Israel bombards northern Gaza and southern Lebanon
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a mosque in the Gaza Strip early Sunday killed at least 19 people, Palestinian officials said, as Israel intensified its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Lebanon in a widening war with Iran-allied militant groups across the region.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New report finds nearly 200% increase in antisemitic incidents in US since Oct. 7 Hamas terror massacre
item.description: The Anti-Defamation League recorded around 10,000 antisemitic incidents since Oct. 7, marking the largest year-to-year increase since the group started recording such incidents in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu calls Macron, other Western leaders who support arms embargo against Israel a ‘disgrace’
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu slammed Western leaders on Saturday who said they support an arms embargo on Israel over how its handling the Gaza war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rising from the ashes: Israelis in nation's war-torn south move home a year after October 7 massacre
item.description: The unique communal structure of the kibbutz plays a vital role in fostering resilience and support as people heal from the devastating impact of the October 7 massacre with many now returning home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Relatives say a whole family was killed in Israel's deadliest West Bank strike since Oct. 7
item.description: TULKAREM, West Bank (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a West Bank cafe that the military said targeted Palestinian militants also killed a family of four, including two young children, relatives told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: NASSER NASSER and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boris Johnson alleges Netanyahu may have bugged his bathroom
item.description: Israel also was accused around the same time of planting listening devices in The White House, The Telegraph also reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: US arranges flights to bring Americans out of Lebanon as others seek escape
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S.-arranged flights have brought about 350 Americans and their immediate relatives out of Lebanon this week during escalated fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, while thousands of others still there face airstrikes and diminishing commercial flights.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MATTHEW LEE and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sanxingdui Ruins were discovered accidentally by a farmer; has since been the site of over 60K relics
item.description: Sanxingdui Ruins is an archaeological site in Guanghan City. Official archaeological excavations have been conducted since 1934 at the site after its discovery in 1929.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: An Israeli airstrike cuts a major highway linking Lebanon with Syria
item.description: An Israeli airstrike has cut off a main highway linking Lebanon with Syria, leaving two huge craters on either side of the road.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Wildlife lover facing charges after allegedly assaulting passersby while attempting to capture a baby seagull
item.description: A Belfast woman was in court Tuesday after allegedly attacking passersby who tried to intervene with her capture of a baby seagull in August.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bus filled with wedding guests plunges into a ravine, killing 7
item.description: A speeding bus filled with wedding guests crashed in southeastern Pakistan on Thursday, killing seven of the passengers while injuring 17 others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 teens detained following explosions near Denmark's Israeli Embassy
item.description: Two Swedish teenagers were detained Thursday on preliminary charges in connection with a pair of explosions near the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New NATO chief Mark Rutte visits Ukraine in his first trip since taking office
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — New NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte visited Ukraine on Thursday in his first official trip since taking office and pledging the alliance's continued support for Kyiv in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel presses forward on two fronts and reports 8 combat deaths as fears of a wider war mount
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel pressed forward on two fronts Wednesday, pursuing a ground incursion into Lebanon against Hezbollah that left eight Israeli soldiers dead and conducting strikes in Gaza that killed dozens, including children.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Three young Swedes were arrested Wednesday in connection with two predawn explosions that occurred in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting a nearby Jewish school to close for the day. Police said no one was injured.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A US bomb from World War II explodes at a Japanese airport, causing a large crater in a taxiway
item.description: Officials said an investigation by the Self-Defense Forces and police confirmed that the explosion was caused by a 500-pound U.S. bomb and there was no further danger.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel, Iran swap threats as strikes on Lebanon continue
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has vowed to retaliate, saying Iran “made a big mistake" by launching a missile attack.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Israel to have 'substantial response' to Iran attacks: John Bolton
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, less than a day after Israel began ground attacks on the militant group Hezbollah in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The new head of NATO vowed on Tuesday to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and expressed confidence that he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance's most powerful member, in November.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, NewsNation
